summary: DHH announces that Dave Thomas (of Pragmatic Programmers fame) will be giving a two-hour presentation on Ruby and Rails at Amazon's Seattle headquarters. The talk will include Active Record tutorials and a full Rails web application demo. DHH expresses excitement that Thomas has quickly grasped the Rails approach and will be presenting using TextMate, an editor DHH contributed to. The post captures an early moment of Rails evangelism at a major tech company.
key_points: Dave Thomas, a respected figure in programming, is presenting Ruby and Rails to Amazon engineers
The presentation includes hands-on Active Record and Rails web application demos, showing Rails being pitched to enterprise audiences early on
DHH takes pride in Thomas using both Rails and TextMate — two projects he contributed to — for the presentation
The post reflects the early grassroots strategy of Rails adoption: respected advocates demoing to engineers at major companies
money_quotes: I'm really impressed how quickly Dave has internalized the Rails approach.
The Amazonians are surely in the hands of a master.
I can't begin to tell you how cool it feels to have one of your all-time technical role models present using not one but two projects I've had a hand in.
key_insight: An early snapshot of Rails evangelism, with DHH celebrating a respected programming authority bringing Rails directly to Amazon's engineers.
